events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need respond with valid JSON object only, no prose. Need assess scenario: Wire pulling feeders & branch industrial site. Observed field condition: exceeded sidewall pressure/jamming (planning item). Work rough-complete. Reference NEC 200.6, 210.5 (identification). Need treat as roughly 70% complete until defects corrected.

We must decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Defect: exceeded sidewall pressure/jamming, likely cable pull planning issue. It might damage conductors/insulation/jacket, lead to non-compliance, safety. Since dangerous/non-compliant? It's a planning item. It can cause cable damage. For assessment, fail due to defect and non-compliant installation. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant maybe. percent_complete: 70 (treat as roughly 70%). risk high/critical? Exceeded sidewall pressure/jamming can damage cables; critical? In heavy industrial feeders, potential for insulation damage, arc fault, fire. So risk high or critical. Probably high. Decision fail.

Need provide findings: pull exceeded allowable sidewall pressure / jam ratio, may have damaged conductor insulation/jacket. Need corrective actions: stop pull, inspect cables with megger/insulation resistance, verify pulling calculations/tension/sw pressure, replace damaged cables, repull per design, recheck identification per NEC 200.6/210.5, re-test.

Work_stage: rough-complete (given). component_conditions: ["installed-defective","non-compliant"]. percent_complete 70. value? Not asked; maybe 0. sound_source irrelevant, event_time, rate maybe 0. order empty. workable empty. rationale brief. references: NEC 200.6, 210.5.

Need ensure JSON only. Should include all fields. Need maybe numeric fields value 0, confidence? Not in schema? It has confidence 0.0. We can set confidence 0.9. event_time 0.0, rate 0.0. order [] workable []. findings/actions.

Let's craft JSON.

events.label: response
events.text: {"decision": "fail", "risk": "high", "work_stage": "rough-complete", "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"], "percent_complete": 70, "value": 0, "sound_source": "", "confidence": 0.9, "event_time": 0.0, "rate": 0.0, "order": [], "workable": [], "findings": ["Wire pull exceeded allowable sidewall pressure/jamming limits during planning/execution", "Potential insulation, jacket, or conductor damage due to improper pull mechanics", "Installation is non-compliant and may not meet engineered pulling calculations and NEC requirements"], "actions": ["Stop pull and do not close up the installation", "Inspect pulled conductors for visible damage and perform insulation-resistance testing per specification", "Recalculate pulling tension, sidewall pressure, and jam ratio against cable manufacturer and project specifications", "Replace any damaged conductors and repull using corrected methods/tension/lube/guide shoes", "Re-verify conductor identification and phasing markings per NEC 200.6 and 210.5", "Re-test and obtain inspection sign-off before closing or energizing"], "rationale": "Exceeding sidewall pressure or jamming limits during a wire pull is a serious installation defect that can damage feeder and branch circuit conductors, creating fire/shock hazards and non-compliance with engineered plans and NEC identification requirements. The installation cannot advance until the defect is corrected and verified.", "references": ["NEC 200.6", "NEC 210.5", "Project engineered drawings and cable-pulling calculations", "Cable manufacturer installation specifications"]}
